content: read reviews/27.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/27.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/27.md.

    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí- **ISSUE (Case determination):** The module docstring says "Erd≈ës asked whether there exists a constant $C > 1$ such that ‚Ä¶" ‚Äî the phrase "asked whether" maps to **Case 1** (yes/no question) per the checklist. The problem was disproved (answer is "no"), so the correct form should be:

# Review: deepmind/27.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 27`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/27`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Œµ-almost covering systems with moduli in [N, CN]).
- [x] Optional: bibliography entry for [FFKPY07] (Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin, Pomerance, Yu).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos27 ... end Erdos27`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos27`).
- [x] `open Finset` and `open scoped Classical` appear **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `HasDistinctModuli`: has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `systemLcm`: has a docstring; marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] `uncoveredDensity`: has a docstring; marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `HasDistinctModuli`.
- [x] Functions returning values use `lowerCamelCase`: `systemLcm`, `uncoveredDensity`.
- N/A: No local notation used (none needed).
- [ ] Optional: no `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for the custom definitions. Consider adding tests to verify `HasDistinctModuli`, `systemLcm`, and `uncoveredDensity` behave as expected on small examples.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problem uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_27` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions.
- [x] Theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source paper ([FFKPY07]).

### `answer()` usage

- **ISSUE (Case determination):** The module docstring says "Erd≈ës asked whether there exists a constant $C > 1$ such that ‚Ä¶" ‚Äî the phrase "asked whether" maps to **Case 1** (yes/no question) per the checklist. The problem was disproved (answer is "no"), so the correct form should be:
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_27 : answer(False) ‚Üî ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶ := by sorry
  ```
  Currently the theorem is stated as a direct negation (Case 3 style):
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_27 : ¬¨ ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶ := by sorry
  ```
  This does not conform to the `answer()` convention for yes/no questions.
- [ ] Should use `answer(False) ‚Üî P` where `P` is the existence claim as originally asked.
- [x] No quantifiers appear as theorem parameters (all quantifiers are inside the theorem body).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (disproved by Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin, Pomerance, Yu ‚Äî not formally verified, consistent with `sorry` in proof body).
- N/A: Not `research open`, so the sorry/open linter check does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (`11`).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number Theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Proof uses multi-line `sorry` format (`by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: Not in `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. Only the main theorem is declared.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (proof body uses 2-space indent; theorem type expression uses alignment-based continuation, standard in Lean 4).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚Ñ§`, `‚Ñï`, `‚Ñù`, `‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚àà`, `‚àß`, `‚Üí`, `¬¨`, `‚â•`, `‚â§`, `Œµ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified as part of this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: FAIL** (1 issue requiring correction, pending build verification)

**Issue requiring fix:**
1. **`answer()` usage (¬ß6):** The original problem is phrased as a yes/no question ("Erd≈ës asked whether ‚Ä¶"), which triggers Case 1. Since the conjecture was disproved, the theorem should use `answer(False) ‚Üî ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶` rather than the current `¬¨ ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶`. This brings the statement into conformance with the `answer()` convention for solved yes/no problems.

**Minor note:**
- Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity-check examples for the three custom definitions (`HasDistinctModuli`, `systemLcm`, `uncoveredDensity`) to verify they behave as expected on small inputs. This is optional per the checklist.

All other checklist items pass. The copyright header, import, module docstring (with bibliography), namespace structure, custom definitions (with docstrings and `noncomputable` where needed), category/AMS attributes, proof body format, and code quality all conform to the required standards.

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 27
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/27](https://www.erdosproblems.com/27)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíAn $\varepsilon$-almost covering system is a set of congruences $a_i \pmod{n_i}$ for distinct
    25‚Üímoduli $n_1 < \cdots < n_k$ such that the natural density of integers satisfying none of them
    26‚Üíis at most $\varepsilon$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíErd≈ës asked whether there exists a constant $C > 1$ such that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and
    29‚Üí$N \geq 1$, there is an $\varepsilon$-almost covering system with $N \leq n_1 < \cdots < n_k \leq CN$.
    30‚ÜíThis was disproved by Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin, Pomerance, and Yu [FFKPY07].
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[FFKPY07] Filaseta, M., Ford, K., Konyagin, S., Pomerance, C., and Yu, G.,
    33‚Üí_Sieving by large integers and covering systems of congruences_.
    34‚ÜíJournal of the American Mathematical Society (2007), 495-517.
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üíopen Finset
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos27
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- A congruence system has distinct moduli if no two pairs share the same modulus. -/
    44‚Üídef HasDistinctModuli (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  S.card = (S.image Prod.snd).card
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- The LCM of all moduli in a congruence system. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def systemLcm (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : ‚Ñï :=
    49‚Üí  (S.image Prod.snd).lcm id
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/--
    52‚ÜíThe density of uncovered integers for a congruence system, measured as the proportion of
    53‚Üíintegers in $\{0, \ldots, L-1\}$ not covered by any congruence, where $L$ is the LCM of
    54‚Üíall moduli.
    55‚Üí-/
    56‚Üínoncomputable def uncoveredDensity (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : ‚Ñù :=
    57‚Üí  let L := systemLcm S
    58‚Üí  ((Finset.range L).filter (fun x =>
    59‚Üí    ‚àÄ p ‚àà S, ¬¨((‚Üëp.2 : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ (‚Üëx - p.1)))).card / (L : ‚Ñù)
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/--
    62‚ÜíThere is no constant $C > 1$ such that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $N \geq 1$, there
    63‚Üíexists an $\varepsilon$-almost covering system whose moduli all lie in the interval $[N, CN]$.
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíErd≈ës conjectured this was possible; it was disproved by Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin,
    66‚ÜíPomerance, and Yu [FFKPY07].
    67‚Üí-/
    68‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    69‚Üítheorem erdos_27 :
    70‚Üí    ¬¨ ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß
    71‚Üí      ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí

    73‚Üí      ‚àÉ S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï),

    78‚Üí        uncoveredDensity S ‚â§ Œµ := by
    79‚Üí  sorry
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üíend Erdos27

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 27

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/27](https://www.erdosproblems.com/27)

An $\varepsilon$-almost covering system is a set of congruences $a_i \pmod{n_i}$ for distinct
moduli $n_1 < \cdots < n_k$ such that the natural density of integers satisfying none of them
is at most $\varepsilon$.

Erd≈ës asked whether there exists a constant $C > 1$ such that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and
$N \geq 1$, there is an $\varepsilon$-almost covering system with $N \leq n_1 < \cdots < n_k \leq CN$.
This was disproved by Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin, Pomerance, and Yu [FFKPY07].

[FFKPY07] Filaseta, M., Ford, K., Konyagin, S., Pomerance, C., and Yu, G.,
_Sieving by large integers and covering systems of congruences_.
Journal of the American Mathematical Society (2007), 495-517.
-/

open Finset

open scoped Classical

namespace Erdos27

/-- A congruence system has distinct moduli if no two pairs share the same modulus. -/
def HasDistinctModuli (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : Prop :=
  S.card = (S.image Prod.snd).card

/-- The LCM of all moduli in a congruence system. -/
noncomputable def systemLcm (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : ‚Ñï :=
  (S.image Prod.snd).lcm id

/--
The density of uncovered integers for a congruence system, measured as the proportion of
integers in $\{0, \ldots, L-1\}$ not covered by any congruence, where $L$ is the LCM of
all moduli.
-/
noncomputable def uncoveredDensity (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : ‚Ñù :=
  let L := systemLcm S
  ((Finset.range L).filter (fun x =>
    ‚àÄ p ‚àà S, ¬¨((‚Üëp.2 : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ (‚Üëx - p.1)))).card / (L : ‚Ñù)

/--
There is no constant $C > 1$ such that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $N \geq 1$, there
exists an $\varepsilon$-almost covering system whose moduli all lie in the interval $[N, CN]$.

Erd≈ës conjectured this was possible; it was disproved by Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin,
Pomerance, and Yu [FFKPY07].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_27 :
    ¬¨ ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß
      ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ N : ‚Ñï, N ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
      ‚àÉ S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï),
        HasDistinctModuli S ‚àß
        S.Nonempty ‚àß
        (‚àÄ p ‚àà S, p.2 ‚â• 2) ‚àß
        (‚àÄ p ‚àà S, N ‚â§ p.2 ‚àß (p.2 : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * N) ‚àß
        uncoveredDensity S ‚â§ Œµ := by
  sorry

end Erdos27

1‚Üí# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíEvery item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4‚Üíwithout an explicit justification.
     5‚Üí
     6‚ÜíSources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ¬ß1‚Äì7),
     7‚Üí[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
     8‚Üí`FormalConjectures/Util/`.
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí---
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí## 1. Copyright Header
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí- [ ] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
    15‚Üí  ```

    17‚Üí  Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
    18‚Üí

    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí  Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    26‚Üí  distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    27‚Üí  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    28‚Üí  See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    29‚Üí  limitations under the License.
    30‚Üí  -/
    31‚Üí  ```
    32‚Üí- [ ] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).

    48‚Üí  *Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/N](https://www.erdosproblems.com/N)
    49‚Üí  ```
    50‚Üí- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    51‚Üí- [ ] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
    52‚Üí

    56‚Üí  - Linter `linter.style.namespace` enforces this; error: *"The declaration '...' is not inside a namespace."*

    58‚Üí- [ ] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces (e.g., `open Filter Nat Real`).
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí## 5. Custom Definitions
    61‚Üí

    63‚Üí- [ ] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
    64‚Üí- [ ] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
    65‚Üí- [ ] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
    66‚Üí- [ ] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (e.g., `normalizedPrimeGap`).
    67‚Üí- [ ] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
    68‚Üí- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.
    69‚Üí

    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí- [ ] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` or `lemma` keyword (not `def` or `example`).
    73‚Üí- [ ] Main theorem is named `erdos_N` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    74‚Üí- [ ] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    75‚Üí- [ ] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line** (e.g., `@[category research open, AMS 11]`).
    76‚Üí- [ ] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    77‚Üí- [ ] Theorem docstrings should ideally reference the source paper(s) (e.g., `[Er95]`, `[AhKh97]`).
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí### `answer()` usage
    80‚Üí
    81‚ÜíThe rule depends on how the **original problem is phrased in English**, not how the
    82‚ÜíLean code currently looks. There are three cases (per `formal-conjectures` README ¬ß6
    83‚Üíand AGENTS.md):
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí**Case 1 ‚Äî Problem asks a yes/no question** ("Does ...", "Are there ...", "Is it true that ...", "Can ...", "asked whether ..."):
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí- [ ] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚Üî P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked:
    88‚Üí  ```lean
    89‚Üí  /-- English: "Does P hold?" -/
    90‚Üí  theorem erdos_N : answer(sorry) ‚Üî P := by sorry
    91‚Üí  ```
    92‚Üí- [ ] If the problem has been solved, replace `answer(sorry)` with `answer(True)` or `answer(False)` accordingly:
    93‚Üí  ```lean

    95‚Üí  theorem erdos_N : answer(True) ‚Üî P := by sorry
    96‚Üí  -- Question answered negatively:
    97‚Üí  theorem erdos_N : answer(False) ‚Üî P := by sorry
    98‚Üí  ```

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ¬ß1‚Äì7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [ ] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
  ```
  /-
  Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
  you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
  You may obtain a copy of the License at

      https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

  Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
  distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
  See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
  limitations under the License.
  -/
  ```
- [ ] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).

## 2. Import Statement

- [ ] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  ```
  import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
  ```
- [ ] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [ ] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [ ] Starts with a heading: `# Erdos Problem N` (or appropriate title).
- [ ] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source, e.g.:
  ```
  *Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/N](https://www.erdosproblems.com/N)
  ```
- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [ ] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.

## 4. Namespace

- [ ] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace ErdosN ... end ErdosN`).
  - Linter `linter.style.namespace` enforces this; error: *"The declaration '...' is not inside a namespace."*
- [ ] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `Erdos42`, not `erdos_42`).
- [ ] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces (e.g., `open Filter Nat Real`).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [ ] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [ ] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
- [ ] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
- [ ] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
- [ ] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (e.g., `normalizedPrimeGap`).
- [ ] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [ ] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` or `lemma` keyword (not `def` or `example`).
- [ ] Main theorem is named `erdos_N` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [ ] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [ ] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line** (e.g., `@[category research open, AMS 11]`).
- [ ] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [ ] Theorem docstrings should ideally reference the source paper(s) (e.g., `[Er95]`, `[AhKh97]`).

### `answer()` usage

The rule depends on how the **original problem is phrased in English**, not how the
Lean code currently looks. There are three cases (per `formal-conjectures` README ¬ß6
and AGENTS.md):

**Case 1 ‚Äî Problem asks a yes/no question** ("Does ...", "Are there ...", "Is it true that ...", "Can ...", "asked whether ..."):

- [ ] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚Üî P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked:
  ```lean
  /-- English: "Does P hold?" -/
  theorem erdos_N : answer(sorry) ‚Üî P := by sorry
  ```
- [ ] If the problem has been solved, replace `answer(sorry)` with `answer(True)` or `answer(False)` accordingly:
  ```lean
  -- Question answered affirmatively:
  theorem erdos_N : answer(True) ‚Üî P := by sorry
  -- Question answered negatively:
  theorem erdos_N : answer(False) ‚Üî P := by sorry
  ```
- [ ] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the **question as originally asked**, so that `answer(True)` means "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no" to that question.

content.thinking:
The review says the theorem should use `answer(False) ‚Üî ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶` instead of the current `¬¨ ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶`.

The original problem asks "whether there exists a constant C > 1 such that..." ‚Äî this is a yes/no question (Case 1). The answer is "no" (disproved), so it should be `answer(False) ‚Üî P` where P is the existence claim.

Let me update the theorem statement.

content.text: The review failed because the theorem uses `¬¨ ‚àÉ C ...` (Case 3 style) instead of `answer(False) ‚Üî ‚àÉ C ...` (Case 1 for a yes/no question that was answered negatively). Let me fix it.

  sorry

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 27

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/27](https://www.erdosproblems.com/27)

An $\varepsilon$-almost covering system is a set of congruences $a_i \pmod{n_i}$ for distinct
moduli $n_1 < \cdots < n_k$ such that the natural density of integers satisfying none of them
is at most $\varepsilon$.

Erd≈ës asked whether there exists a constant $C > 1$ such that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and
$N \geq 1$, there is an $\varepsilon$-almost covering system with $N \leq n_1 < \cdots < n_k \leq CN$.
This was disproved by Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin, Pomerance, and Yu [FFKPY07].

[FFKPY07] Filaseta, M., Ford, K., Konyagin, S., Pomerance, C., and Yu, G.,
_Sieving by large integers and covering systems of congruences_.
Journal of the American Mathematical Society (2007), 495-517.
-/

open Finset

open scoped Classical

namespace Erdos27

/-- A congruence system has distinct moduli if no two pairs share the same modulus. -/
def HasDistinctModuli (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : Prop :=
  S.card = (S.image Prod.snd).card

/-- The LCM of all moduli in a congruence system. -/
noncomputable def systemLcm (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : ‚Ñï :=
  (S.image Prod.snd).lcm id

/--
The density of uncovered integers for a congruence system, measured as the proportion of
integers in $\{0, \ldots, L-1\}$ not covered by any congruence, where $L$ is the LCM of
all moduli.
-/
noncomputable def uncoveredDensity (S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï)) : ‚Ñù :=
  let L := systemLcm S
  ((Finset.range L).filter (fun x =>
    ‚àÄ p ‚àà S, ¬¨((‚Üëp.2 : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ (‚Üëx - p.1)))).card / (L : ‚Ñù)

/--
There is no constant $C > 1$ such that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $N \geq 1$, there
exists an $\varepsilon$-almost covering system whose moduli all lie in the interval $[N, CN]$.

Erd≈ës conjectured this was possible; it was disproved by Filaseta, Ford, Konyagin,
Pomerance, and Yu [FFKPY07].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_27 :
    ¬¨ ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß
      ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ N : ‚Ñï, N ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
      ‚àÉ S : Finset (‚Ñ§ √ó ‚Ñï),
        HasDistinctModuli S ‚àß
        S.Nonempty ‚àß
        (‚àÄ p ‚àà S, p.2 ‚â• 2) ‚àß
        (‚àÄ p ‚àà S, N ‚â§ p.2 ‚àß (p.2 : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * N) ‚àß
        uncoveredDensity S ‚â§ Œµ := by
  sorry

end Erdos27

content.text: Done. Changed the theorem from `¬¨ ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶` to `answer(False) ‚Üî ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 1 ‚àß ‚Ä¶`, conforming to Case 1 of the `answer()` convention for yes/no questions answered negatively.
